serviceNotes.engine oil: Dacia’s European maintenance programme aligns with Renault Group intervals: routine maintenance including oil and filter at 20,000 km or 12 months (whichever first) per the official Sandero maintenance booklet / myDacia service schedule.
serviceNotes.cabin filter: Pollen filter replacement is performed at the scheduled maintenance interval for the model.
serviceNotes.air filter: Engine air filter replacement is specified at 60,000 km / 4 years per the official Dacia maintenance schedule.
serviceNotes.coolant flush: Budget models still need coolant inhibitor renewal long-term; 120,000 km is a reasonable preventive exchange interval.
serviceNotes.brake fluid: Dacia specifies brake fluid replacement every 120,000 km or 4 years per the official maintenance schedule.
serviceNotes.spark plugs: Spark plug replacement is specified at 60,000 km / 4 years for petrol engines in the Dacia maintenance programme.
serviceNotes.timing belt: Dacia specifies timing belt replacement at 120,000 km or 6 years, whichever comes first. Failure is catastrophic.
serviceNotes.auxiliary belt: Auxiliary drive belt replacement is specified at 120,000 km or 6 years in the official Dacia schedule.
serviceNotes.check brake pads: Brake pad inspection is performed at each scheduled maintenance visit.
